name: Pickled Hot Peppers (Wwrj17a)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut two small slits in each pepper. You may want to wear gloves to prevent burning hands.
2. Dissolve the pickling salt in 4 quarts of water.
3. Pour the mixture over the peppers and let stand for 12 to 18 hours in a cool place.
4. Drain, rinse thoroughly, and drain again.
5. Combine 2 cups of water with all remaining ingredients except honey; simmer for 15 minutes, then add honey.
6. Remove garlic from the liquid.
7. Pack the peppers into hot jars, leaving 1/4-inch headspace.
8. Pour boiling hot pickling liquid over the peppers, leaving 1/4-inch headspace.
9. Adjust lids and process half-pints and pints for 10 minutes in a boiling water bath.
Yield: 16 half-pints or 8 pints.
